_README: Bipolar contrastive prototypes for the Code Biology criteria embedding axis. DOMAIN-GENERAL, BALANCED-EXEMPLAR design (2026-06-17, rev 2): the axis measures *code biology in general*, not the genetic code in particular, mirroring the §9.1 judge rewrite. NEUTRALITY IS BY BALANCE, NOT ABSTRACTION: each pole is one domain-neutral definitional ANCHOR plus a basket of CONCRETE instances spread across domains (genetic, neural, semiotic/language, signalling, immune/olfactory). The genetic code appears as ONE instance among several, so the pooled (mean) pole is not pulled onto the molecular archetype; because the per-instance topicalities are diverse they partially cancel in the mean, leaving 'code-ness' as the shared direction (the §4 shared-strength lever keeps it). Concreteness is kept deliberately high -- decoder-only embedders (harrier) represent abstract relational prose weakly, which would narrow the poles. Each criterion keeps its OWN theoretical opposite (a distinct axis), NOT pooled into a generic 'mere physics' blob: two_worlds neg = one continuous system / no second world; adaptors neg = direct contact / no third mediator; arbitrariness neg = physically determined / could not be otherwise. The historic stereochemical-theory claim is retained as ONE determinist instance (it is the canonical counter-argument), balanced by neural / self-assembly instances. adaptors<->arbitrariness stay theoretically coupled (mediator presence is the fingerprint of arbitrariness) so their negative axes legitimately correlate. PRIOR PROVENANCE (2026-06-14): positives were corpus-mined molecular sentences from www.codebiology.org/index.html + glossary.html, negatives register-matched from its 'life is chemistry' / stereochemical passages; that molecular framing is what this revision generalises. instruct_* are harrier query instructions ('Instruct: {task}\nQuery: {passage}'); paper text is embedded as a document with NO instruction. Watch pole_separation['within'] after the next pole re-embed; edit freely.
_controls_README: ILLUSTRATIVE AGREE/DISAGREE anchors for the LLM judge (criteria_judge.build_chunk_prompt), NOT embedding poles. Restored 2026-06-17 (the rev-2 pole rewrite dropped them; judge_pilot.py needs them). Per §9.1 these are framed as illustrative of the abstract relation, the molecular genetic code being the exemplar, not the required form.

_controls.genetic_code_positive: In the genetic code, codons and amino acids are two independent molecular worlds bridged by transfer-RNA adaptors, and the assignment of codons to amino acids is an arbitrary convention that could be otherwise without breaking any law of chemistry.
_controls.deterministic_chemistry_negative: Enzyme specificity arises from stereochemical lock-and-key complementarity: the substrate binds the active site for steric and thermodynamic reasons, the reaction is a direct chemical transformation, and the outcome is physically determined rather than conventional.

two_worlds.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that the phenomenon involves two independent worlds of objects with no necessary physical link between them
two_worlds.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the phenomenon is a single continuous physical process in which the parts are directly and necessarily linked
pos: A code is a set of rules that map the objects of one independent world onto the objects of another, where nothing in either world's intrinsic nature dictates the correspondence.
There is no necessary chemical link between codons and amino acids; the two form separate molecular worlds bridged only by the rules of the genetic code.
A sensory stimulus and the neural response it evokes belong to two distinct domains, and which response stands for which stimulus is not fixed by their physics.
Signs and meanings make up two separate worlds: a word and the thing it denotes share nothing intrinsic, so only a convention relates them.
In signal transduction an extracellular cue and the intracellular program it triggers are independent worlds, related by the cell's interpretation rather than by direct physical necessity.
An odorant and the perception it elicits are independent worlds; the same molecule could in principle be mapped to a different percept.
neg: Codons and amino acids are linked directly by stereochemical affinity, forming a single continuous chemical system rather than two independent worlds.
When a polymer assembles spontaneously, its sequence is fixed by the interactions among its own subunits; there is no second, independent world to map onto.
A reflex is one continuous causal chain from stimulus to response, with no separate world of meanings interposed between them.
The reaction proceeds along a single physically obligatory pathway, one state transforming directly into the next, all within one world.
The molecules interact through their own complementary surfaces, so the whole process stays within a single physical world.
Everything reduces to one world whose behaviour is dictated end to end by physical law; positing a separate, independent world adds nothing.

adaptors.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that a third class of mediator physically bridges two independent worlds
adaptors.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the two parties interact directly, by catalysis or complementarity, with no mediating third entity
pos: Two independent worlds can be related only through a third entity, a mediator, that reads one world and acts on the other; its presence is the fingerprint of a code.
Transfer-RNAs act as adaptors in protein synthesis, recognising a codon at one end and carrying the corresponding amino acid at the other.
The nervous system is the mediator of a neural code, transforming a pattern of stimulation into a pattern of response that it alone assigns.
A decoder or interpretant stands between sign and meaning and executes the mapping that neither world fixes on its own.
Antigen-presenting molecules mediate between a pathogen and the immune response, bridging two worlds that have no direct physical link.

A distinct third party does the joining: a mediator that touches one world at one end and the other world at the other end, so the two never meet directly and the mediator alone fixes which goes with which.
neg: Codons pair with amino acids directly through their own stereochemical affinity, with no adaptor mediating between them.
An enzyme merely accelerates the transformation of substrate into product; it speeds a reaction rather than mapping one independent world onto another.
Two strands recognise each other by direct template complementarity, without any intervening bridging molecule.
A ligand binds its receptor lock-and-key, and that direct fit alone determines the outcome, with no separate mediator assigning it.
The stimulus drives the response directly along a hardwired pathway, with no interpreter standing between them.
The partners contact each other directly and that contact alone determines the outcome; there is no third go-between anywhere in the process.

arbitrariness.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that the coding rules are arbitrary conventions, compatible with but not dictated by physical law, and could in principle be otherwise
arbitrariness.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the correspondence is dictated by physical structure, affinity or thermodynamic necessity and could not be otherwise
pos: In science, arbitrariness means independence from physical necessity: a mapping is arbitrary when its rules are compatible with the laws of physics but not dictated by them, and could in principle be otherwise.
Any codon can in principle be assigned to any amino acid; rewiring a transfer-RNA makes it deliver a different amino acid, proving the assignment is not fixed by chemistry.
Just as any letter can be mapped to any pattern of dots and dashes, a code's rules are one small selection from an unlimited set of possible alternatives.
The correspondence between a stimulus and its neural response is conventional rather than forced: the same input could be encoded by a different pattern without violating any physical law.
The link between a word and its meaning is a historically frozen convention; nothing physical compels that particular pairing.
The relationship is not determined by intrinsic affinity but selected from many physically possible mappings, so it could have been otherwise.
neg: On the stereochemical theory the codon–amino-acid correspondence follows directly from molecular structure and affinity, so it could not be otherwise.
The pairing is fixed for structural reasons: geometry and binding energy permit exactly one assignment, leaving no freedom of choice.
The neural response is the lawful physical consequence of the stimulus; given the system's biophysics, no alternative mapping is possible.
When a structure assembles spontaneously, its organisation is completely determined by the interactions among its own parts, leaving no room for convention.
Direct complementarity makes the outcome obligatory; it is physically determined, not chosen.
There is exactly one possible outcome and physics fixes it, so the correspondence is necessary rather than conventional.
